Name: Light Shafts
Description: Simulates volumetric light shafts (god rays) produced when light interacts with atmospheric scattering and occlusion. Uses sky masking, directional filtering, and blur accumulation to approximate volumetric scattering in screen space.

Fields.Name: Enable Light Shafts
Fields.Description: Enables or disables volumetric light shaft rendering. When disabled, no god ray or volumetric light scattering is applied.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Angle
Fields.Description: Direction of the light shaft effect, typically derived from sun or key light orientation in screen space.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Enable Reference Light
Fields.Description: Enables use of a reference light source for generating light shaft direction and intensity instead of global lighting.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Light Bank ID
Fields.Description: Selects which light bank (group of lights) is used as the source for light shaft generation.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: High Quality Sky Mask
Fields.Description: Enables higher precision sky masking to reduce artifacts and improve separation between sky and occluders.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Mask Depth Threshold
Fields.Description: Depth threshold used to determine whether a pixel contributes to light shaft occlusion.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Mask Size Angle
Fields.Description: Controls angular size of the sky mask region influencing light shaft spread and falloff.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Mask Center Power
Fields.Description: Biases sky mask intensity toward the center, controlling concentration of volumetric rays.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Multiply Color
Fields.Description: Multiplicative color applied to sky contribution in the light shaft calculation.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Add Color
Fields.Description: Additive color contribution applied to sky-based light shaft scattering.

Fields.Name: Exposure Influence Rate: Add Color
Fields.Description: Controls how much scene exposure affects additive color contribution in light shafts.

Fields.ID: GS Presents - Parallel mode begin ang
Fields.Name: Parallel Mode: Start Angle
Fields.Description: Start angle defining the range in which light shafts are treated as parallel (directionally uniform rays).

Fields.ID: GS Presents - Parallel mode end ang
Fields.Name: Parallel Mode: End Angle
Fields.Description: End angle for parallel light shaft behavior, transitioning into radial or non-parallel scattering.

Fields.ID: GS Presents - Fade out begin ang
Fields.Name: Fade Out: Start Angle
Fields.Description: Angle at which light shaft intensity begins to fade based on view or light direction.

Fields.ID: GS Presents - Fade out end ang
Fields.Name: Fade Out: End Angle
Fields.Description: Angle at which light shafts are fully faded out.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Blur Range
Fields.Description: Controls spatial extent of blur applied to light shafts, affecting ray softness and spread.

Fields.ID: GS Presents - LightShaft ShaftBlurRange for Parallel
Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Shaft Blur Range for Parallel
Fields.Description: Blur range specifically used when light shafts are in parallel mode.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Shaft Blur Falloff
Fields.Description: Controls how quickly light shaft blur diminishes with distance or sampling steps.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Shaft Blur Count
Fields.Description: Number of blur samples used to construct volumetric light shafts. Higher values improve quality but reduce performance.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Enable Light Shafts
Fields.Description: Enables the Yebis light shaft rendering pipeline for volumetric ray and glare simulation.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Color Scale
Fields.Description: Scales the final color intensity of light shafts and volumetric scattering contributions.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Shaft Length
Fields.Description: Controls the apparent length and extension of volumetric light rays.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Glare Ratio
Fields.Description: Adjusts the amount of glare blended into light shafts, affecting bloom-like intensity around bright sources.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Angle Attenuation
Fields.Description: Controls how strongly light shafts fade based on viewing angle relative to the light source.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Noise Mask
Fields.Description: Controls procedural noise masking used to break up banding and add natural variation to light shafts.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Noise Frequency
Fields.Description: Adjusts the scale and frequency of procedural noise patterns used in volumetric scattering.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Light Shaft Angle
Fields.Description: Directional angle of Yebis light shaft projection in screen or world space.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Light Shaft Radial: X
Fields.Description: Horizontal radial spread of the light shaft effect.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Light Shaft Radial: Y
Fields.Description: Vertical radial spread of the light shaft effect.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Light Shaft Distance: Z
Fields.Description: Depth-based distance scaling for light shaft projection and attenuation.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Diffraction Ring
Fields.Description: Controls the intensity of diffraction ring artifacts generated around bright light sources.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Diffraction Ring Radius
Fields.Description: Defines the radius or size of diffraction ring patterns around bright highlights.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Diffraction Ring Attenuation
Fields.Description: Controls how quickly diffraction ring intensity fades outward from the light source.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Diffraction Ring Spectrum Order
Fields.Description: Adjusts spectral ordering and color separation within diffraction ring patterns.

Fields.Name: Yebis: Ring Outer Color
Fields.Description: Color tint applied to the outer region of diffraction rings and light scattering halos.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Mask Mode
Fields.Description: Selects the algorithm used to compute sky masking for occlusion of light shafts.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Color Saturation
Fields.Description: Controls saturation of sky colors contributing to light shafts, affecting vibrancy of rays.

Fields.Name: Light Shaft: Sky Color Threshold
Fields.Description: Threshold that determines which sky brightness values contribute to light shaft generation.
